_meta.description: Parametric panel interior definition schema. Any panel variant is described by dividing the universal frame into zones. From this schema, all outputs generate: SVG, Three.js, STL, cut lists, cost.
_meta.frame: universal — 1000mm wide × height mm tall, T-profile. Short T-bar webs protrude outward (up at top, down at bottom) for bolt connections.

frame_orientation_rules.description: CRITICAL — these rules define how T-bars are oriented in the panel frame. Reference this before generating ANY view.

side_tbars.flange: faces OUTWARD (panel exterior face, contacts adjacent panel)
side_tbars.protrusion: NONE — flush at top and bottom, no web extends beyond panel height

short_tbars.flange: spans panel width (1000mm), centered on frame depth (60mm = flange width). Sits INSIDE the panel at the top/bottom edge.
short_tbars.web: spans 990mm (10mm less than panel width), centered (5mm gap each side). Points OUTWARD from the panel. Top web UP, bottom web DOWN. The 5mm gap at each side allows the connector T-bar web to drop through at inline panel junctions.
short_tbars.protrusion: The WEB extending outside the panel body. Top: web goes UP. Bottom: web goes DOWN. Protrusion height = web_height (e.g. 53mm for T60×60).

protrusion_direction.CORRECT: Webs point OUTWARD: top T-bar web extends UP above panel, bottom T-bar web extends DOWN below panel. Web is 990mm wide, centered (5mm gap each side).
protrusion_direction.WRONG_1: Protrusions do NOT extend left/right beyond the panel width
protrusion_direction.WRONG_2: The web is NOT 1000mm — it is 990mm, leaving a 10mm gap at each panel junction for the connector T-bar web
protrusion_direction.WRONG_3: The web does NOT point inward into the panel — it points outward
protrusion_direction.view_from_face: Top: flange at panel top edge, web extending upward (990mm). Bottom: flange at panel bottom edge, web extending downward (990mm). 5mm visible gap at each side.
protrusion_direction.view_from_top: Flange visible (60mm wide, centered on wall depth). Web thin line at center of flange going up/down (into page).
protrusion_direction.view_from_side: T cross-section visible: flange (horizontal, 60mm) with web (vertical, 53mm) extending outward from panel.

vertical_wall_connection_A.description: Vertical Wall Connection A — double T-bar connector (I-shape). Two T-bars with flanges connected (wide parts facing each other), webs pointing outward. Always mounted on the OUTSIDE (exterior) surface of the wall web.
vertical_wall_connection_A.use_case: All OUTSIDE walls (exterior + corner walls). NOT used on interior walls (separate solution TBD). NOT used where junction cube is present.
vertical_wall_connection_A.rule: If the wall is an outside wall AND not at a multi-directional junction (cube), use VWC-A. Always on the exterior face.
vertical_wall_connection_A.profile: Same T-bar profile as frame (e.g. T60×60)

assembly.lower_web: Sits against outside face of both panel webs. Bolted through with 2-4 M12 bolts per side.
assembly.flanges_connected: Two T-bar flanges (wide parts) face each other. Welded or screwed together (TBD). The flanges are in the CENTER of the I-shape.
assembly.upper_web_ridge: Extends outward (up for top, down for bottom). Creates a RIDGE — connection point for upper floor wall panels. The next floor's wall panel bottom web bolts to this ridge.

dimensions_T60.total_height: 120mm (53mm lower web + 7mm lower flange + 7mm upper flange + 53mm upper web)

vertical_wall_connection_A.bolt_holes: Both webs have bolt holes with the same spacing pattern. Lower web: bolts through to panel web. Upper web (ridge): bolts for upper floor wall panel bottom web.
vertical_wall_connection_A.structural_function: 1) Lower web transfers shear across inline panel joint. 2) Upper web (ridge) provides bolt interface for next floor's wall panels — continuous vertical load path through floors.

post_assembly_infill.description: After VWC-A bolts are installed and upper panels placed, the connector zone is filled with poured borate mortar. This transforms the steel-only connection into a composite steel-masonry ring beam (viga de amarre).
post_assembly_infill.benefit: 4.2× vertical capacity, continuous seismic shear transfer, monolithic building behavior
post_assembly_infill.same_for: VWC-A, VWC-I90, VWC-C, TWC-A, TWC-I90, TWC-C — all connector types get infill

top_wall_connection_A.description: Top Wall Connection A — single T-bar connector. Used at the TOPMOST panel ring only (roof level). Lower web bolts to panel web (same as VWC-A lower half). Flange provides a flat bearing surface for the roof plate/eave beam. No upper web/ridge — no next floor above.
top_wall_connection_A.use_case: Top ring of ALL exterior walls. Replaces VWC-A where there is no upper floor. Also TWC-I90 and TWC-C variants for T-junctions and corners at the top ring.
top_wall_connection_A.rule: If this is the topmost panel ring AND exterior wall, use TWC instead of VWC.

assembly.web: Sits against outside face of both panel webs. Bolted through with 2-4 M12 bolts per side.
assembly.flange: Wide part faces UP. Creates a flat bearing surface for roof plate beam.

top_wall_connection_A.bolt_holes: Web has bolt holes matching panel web pattern.
top_wall_connection_A.structural_function: 1) Web transfers shear across inline panel joint at roof level. 2) Flange provides flat bearing for roof plate beam — clean eave line.

post_assembly_infill.description: After VWC-A bolts are installed and upper panels placed, the connector zone is filled with poured borate mortar. This transforms the steel-only connection into a composite steel-masonry ring beam (viga de amarre).
post_assembly_infill.benefit: 4.2× vertical capacity, continuous seismic shear transfer, monolithic building behavior
post_assembly_infill.same_for: VWC-A, VWC-I90, VWC-C, TWC-A, TWC-I90, TWC-C — all connector types get infill

variants.TWC-A: inline (same as VWC-A but single T-bar)
variants.TWC-I90: T-junction (same as VWC-I90 but single T-bar)
variants.TWC-C: corner (same as VWC-C but single T-bar)

inside_wall_connection_A.description: Inside Wall Connection A — single T-bar piece, flange (wide part) on top, web bolted to panel web face. Simpler than VWC-A: no ridge needed since interior walls don't carry floor loads from above.
inside_wall_connection_A.profile: Same T-bar profile as frame (e.g. T60×60)

assembly.web: Sits against outside face of both panel webs at junction. Bolted through with 2-4 M12 bolts per side.
assembly.flange: Wide part faces outward (up for top, down for bottom). Caps the junction.

inside_wall_connection_A.bolt_holes: Same spacing pattern as panel web bolt holes.
inside_wall_connection_A.structural_function: Transfers shear across inline interior wall panel joint. No vertical load path needed (floor loads carried by exterior walls).

diagonal_bracing.note: X-pattern diagonal braces — one per side of web, opposing directions

diagonal_thickness.note: sits flat against web, vertical strips bow over it

opening.description: Empty opening (door, window frame without glass)

x.note: offset from left edge of panel

constraints.note: minimum 60mm (one flange width) of solid material around any opening

lattice.description: Slats (teak or bamboo) with gaps — ventilation, light filtering, privacy

slat_count.note: derived from zone width and slat spacing

curve.description: Curved bamboo surface freely placed and rotated within the panel. Defined by start point, end point, and bezier control points in panel-local coordinates (origin = bottom-left of panel). Multiple curves can overlap, intersect, or stack.

start.note: curve start point in panel coordinates

end.note: curve end point in panel coordinates

rotation.note: rotation around curve midpoint. 0=as defined, 90=vertical arch, 45=diagonal

anchor_start.note: what the start point connects to. frame=panel T-bar, divider=internal T-bar, free=floating in mortar

control_points.note: 1 point = simple arc (quadratic bezier), 2 points = S-curve or asymmetric (cubic bezier), 0 points = straight line

ties.note: tension members connecting curve endpoints. Only needed when curve creates thrust (arch). Not needed for S-curves or decorative curves.

cross_pieces.note: perpendicular supports that force bamboo into the defined curve shape. Heights derived from the bezier curve at evenly spaced positions.

node.description: Structural point element where curves meet frame, dividers, or each other. Transfers forces between elements. Typically a solid block (teak, hardwood, concrete, steel).

connects.note: list of elements that meet at this node — curves, ties, frame T-bars, dividers

functions.note: what this node does structurally or functionally

faces.description: Each face of the node block can have holes, notches, or channels. Defined per face for full parametric control.

through.note: true = notch goes all the way through the block

angle.note: rotation of the notch profile, e.g. 45° for diagonal strip entry

notch.purpose: diagonal strip entry, strip seating, web engagement, cross-piece bearing

channel.purpose: conduit routing, strip guide, drainage, mortar flow

slot.purpose: strip passage, web engagement, clamping strip seating

divider_node.note: where two arches meet at an internal divider — receives thrust from both sides (cancels)

features.purpose: bamboo strip seating — strips rest in slots
diagonal brace entry (60×20mm) — notch reaches from web-facing side to top/bottom surface of block
diagonal brace entry (60×20mm) — notch reaches from web-facing side to top/bottom surface of block

outer.description: face away from web, where clamp strip sits

features.purpose: M5 clamping screws — pass through clamp strip → bamboo strips → HDPE → into flange. Spaced every 70mm along the 1000mm panel width (every 2nd bamboo strip).

inner.note: no features — flat bearing surface against flange

hdpe_spacer_block.strip_anchor_y_note: mm from top of block — where the clamping screw holds bamboo strips. This is the start/end point of the bowed vertical strips. Must match outer face screw hole y position.
hdpe_spacer_block.note: material interchangeable: hdpe, teak, hardwood, recycled plastic. Same geometry, different material properties. Screw holes on outer face for clamping strip attachment.

derives_from_curve: thrust = f(load, span, rise) — calculated from curve geometry
structural_properties.note: structural calculations auto-generated from curve definition

panel_definition.description: A panel is defined by its zones. Zones can be stacked vertically (wall panels) or placed side by side with internal T-bar dividers (floor panels).

zones.note: for wall panels: zones stack vertically within the frame

dividers.note: internal T-bar divider (same profile as frame, same 1120mm piece)

clerestory.name: Solid below + glass above (knee wall replacement)

bathroom_with_high_window.name: Solid wall with ventilation window at top

floor_5m_double_arch.name: Floor panel, 5m span, double arch with central divider

floor_10m_quad_arch.name: Floor panel, 10m span, 4 arches with 3 dividers (bridge/demonstration)

vault_ceiling_panel.name: Wall panel with vaulted interior surface (decorative ceiling effect)
vault_ceiling_panel.note: deep curve creates barrel vault look from inside. Panel is still flat on exterior.

asymmetric_floor.name: Floor panel with one end higher (ramp, sloped terrace)
asymmetric_floor.note: end heights differ — creates a gentle slope while maintaining arch structure

mixed_curve_floor_8m.name: 8m floor panel: deep arch center + shallow arches at supports
mixed_curve_floor_8m.note: non-uniform segment lengths — wider center span where loads are lower, shorter spans near supports where shear is highest

catenary_bridge_12m.name: 12m bridge panel: 4 segments with catenary curves for optimal compression
catenary_bridge_12m.note: outer segments are asymmetric (ramped), inner segments are symmetric (maximum span). Each segment has independent curve parameters.

compound_wall_panel.name: Wall panel with curved niche (built-in shelf or arch detail)

zones.note: arch-shaped niche in wall, decorative or functional shelf

compound_wall_panel.note: solid below, curved niche in the middle, solid above. The curve creates an arched recess in the wall surface.

mixed_solid_and_lattice.name: Solid lower half + ventilation lattice upper (tropical climate)

clamp.description: Clamping strip that holds bamboo strips against the frame web. Runs along T-bar length.

tooth_pitch.note: for v_tooth type — teeth interlock with bamboo strips

fastener.description: Screws, bolts, dowels — any discrete fastening element. Parametric size and type.

grade.note: e.g. 8.8 for structural bolts, 4.8 for general, n/a for dowels

drives_into.note: what material the fastener goes into — steel, teak, hdpe, mortar

spacing_rule.description: When fasteners repeat along a line (e.g. screws holding a clamp strip), define by spacing rather than individual positions

clamp_screw_M5.head: pan
clamp_screw_M5.note: M5 every 70mm (every 2nd bamboo strip), holds V-profile clamp to web

frame_bolt_M12.head: hex

pattern.layout: 2 bolts per side edge, horizontally spaced near left and right panel edges
pattern.note: 1.5 × bolt diameter from panel side edge
pattern.spacing_note: 3 × bolt diameter between bolt centers in each pair

frame_bolt_M12.note: standard panel-to-connector-ring bolt — 4 per protrusion (2 pairs near side edges)

kigumi_pin_teak.head: none
kigumi_pin_teak.note: komisen — wooden pin securing Japanese timber joint

kigumi_pin_guadua.head: none
kigumi_pin_guadua.note: guadua dowel shrinks slightly after insertion — tightens over time

connector.description: The connector is a parametric layer stack between panel webs. Replaces the fixed T-bar connector ring. Any combination of materials, fastened with a single through-bolt/screw. Both visible faces can be finished materials (teak, guadua).

items.material: teak | guadua | steel_tbar | hdpe | hardwood | concrete
items.role: exterior_face | wall_web | connector_body | floor_web | interior_face | bearing_plate
items.visible: boolean — is this layer an exposed finish surface?
items.source: panel_protrusion (for web layers) or cut_piece (for boards)

fastener.length: through_all (calculated from sum of layer thicknesses)
fastener.head: hex | countersunk | washer_head
fastener.positions: same parametric bolt hole rule as panels

steel_tbar_connector.description: Original design — steel T-bar turned 90 degrees

v1_teak_plus_steel_connector.description: V1 default — steel T-bar connector PLUS teak boards for bearing and finish. Belt and suspenders.

layers.note: T-bar turned 90deg, same as original design
sits on wall flange, distributes floor load
visible as ceiling from room below

v1_teak_plus_steel_connector.notes: V1 overengineered: steel T-bar handles structure, teak boards add bearing surface + finished faces. Future option: drop the T-bar once testing proves teak alone is sufficient.

future_all_wood_connector.description: Future option — teak boards only, no steel T-bar. Pending structural validation.
future_all_wood_connector.status: future — requires testing to validate without steel T-bar

guadua_connector.description: Low-cost — guadua culm with routed channel

bearing_plate_connector.description: Heavy floor loads — wide bearing plate distributes load

layers.note: overhangs wall flange for maximum bearing area

connector.structural_note: For the 10m bridge panel (1360 kg): bearing stress on a 60mm teak board = 0.11 MPa. Teak perpendicular compressive strength = 11 MPa. Safety factor = 100×. Even a 30mm board is vastly overengineered for floor loads.

junction_cube.description: Universal multi-directional junction node. Handles any junction where 3+ panels meet from different directions (L-corners, T-junctions, floor-wall intersections). The layer-stack connector handles inline 2-panel joints; the junction cube handles everything else.
junction_cube.problem_solved: Where two outer wall panels, an inner wall at 90°, and a floor panel all meet — the layer-stack connector cannot receive webs from 3+ directions.

geometry.note: Each face independently receives one panel web. Unused faces stay empty. Any combination of faces can be active.

steel.description: Solid or hollow steel cube, drill and tap all 6 faces
steel.use_case: Structural junctions, multi-story, high-load floor intersections

teak.description: Solid teak block, pre-drilled for dowel pins
teak.note: Dowel pins shrink after insertion — self-tightening joint

concrete.description: Cast concrete cube with embedded threaded inserts
concrete.note: Future option — requires insert placement during casting

L_corner.example: Exterior wall end meets perpendicular exterior wall

T_junction.example: Two co-planar outer wall panels + one interior wall at 90°

T_junction_with_floor.description: The unsolved case — T-wall junction at floor level
T_junction_with_floor.example: Two outer wall panels + inner wall at 90° + floor panel from below

face_assignment.face_1: outer wall panel 1 (bottom web bolts to cube top or side)
face_assignment.face_2: outer wall panel 2 (bottom web bolts to adjacent face)
face_assignment.face_3: inner wall panel (bottom web bolts to perpendicular face)
face_assignment.face_4: floor panel (side web bolts to remaining face)

floor_wall_simple.description: Single wall panel sitting on floor panel
floor_wall_simple.note: Could also use layer-stack connector, but cube works if other panels join later

junction_cube.design_philosophy: One cube fits all junctions — same as one frame fits all panels. A workshop batch-produces cubes and ships to site. No junction-specific fabrication. Only the bolt/dowel count per face changes based on load.
junction_cube.parametric: Cube scales with profile: T60→53mm, T100→93mm, T150→143mm. Bolt pattern scales proportionally.

from_panel_definition: SVG cross-section drawing (with dimensions, materials labeled)
SVG elevation view (as placed in building)
Material cut list (steel lengths, bamboo strip count, mortar volume)
Structural capacity (for floor panels: load per m²)
generation_outputs.note: One JSON panel definition → all outputs generated automatically. No manual drawing needed.
